page: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable performance instance built on the AWS Nitro System. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor operating at 2.5 GHz. The instance is configured with 4 vCPUs, derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. It includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware is bundled with this instance type, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps.

Benchmark data positions the t3.xlarge in the lower performance tiers. In CPU-intensive tasks, both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests rate the instance in the bottom 25%. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and multi-core scores fall into the bottom 10%, reflecting its burstable CPU allocation model. Memory bandwidth tests for both small cached blocks and large uncached blocks consistently rank in the bottom 25%. Cryptographic and compression workloads, such as gzip and bzip3 decompression, also show weak to poor performance profiles.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the t3.xlarge offers a budget-friendly entry point for workloads that experience periodic spikes in demand rather than continuous high CPU utilization. Because CPU resources are burstable, it is qualitatively cost-effective for development environments, low-traffic web servers, code repositories, and microservices. However, the lack of local storage and lower benchmark scores in database operations and LLM inference make it less suitable for sustained high-throughput databases or intensive data science tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable performance instance utilizing the AWS Nitro hypervisor and an x86_64 Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 2.5 GHz. It features 4 vCPUs across 2 physical cores, 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps, with no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results place the instance in the lower performance tiers, with stress-ng, Geekbench, and memory bandwidth tests consistently scoring in the bottom 10% to 25%. This performance profile reflects its burstable CPU allocation model, which is designed for workloads with variable resource demands. Qualitatively, the t3.xlarge provides a cost-effective solution for non-continuous workloads. It is best suited for general-purpose applications, development environments, code repositories, and low-traffic web serving where sustained maximum CPU performance is not required.
og_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps network baseline, designed for cost-effective handling of temporary workload spikes.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services t3.xlarge is a burstable x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 1 Gbps baseline network speed.
tagline: A burstable, budget-friendly virtual server designed to handle temporary CPU spikes for development environments and low-traffic web applications.
bullet_points: Burstable CPU allocation with 4 vCPUs and 2 physical Intel Xeon cores
16.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz
Baseline network bandwidth of 1 Gbps on the Nitro hypervisor
No bundled local storage or GPU hardware
Best suited for development environments, code repositories, and low-traffic web serving
